description: ETag conditional GET returns 304 Not Modified
304 response includes ETag header
Weak ETag comparison for GET
If-Modified-Since with future date ignored
If-Modified-Since with garbage date ignored
If-None-Match takes precedence over If-Modified-Since
If-None-Match with unquoted ETag
If-None-Match: * on existing resource returns 304
Last-Modified conditional GET returns 304 Not Modified
405 response must include an Allow header
Absolute-form request-target — server should accept per RFC
Unrecognized Accept value — server may return 406 or default representation
Asterisk-form (*) request-target with GET must be rejected
Valid GET request — confirms server is reachable
Valid single-chunk POST must be accepted
Zero-length chunked body (just terminator) must be accepted
Chunk extension (valid per RFC) — server should accept or may reject
Chunk size with uppercase hex (A = 10) should be accepted
Valid multi-chunk POST must be accepted
Chunked body without zero terminator — incomplete transfer
Valid chunked body with trailer field should be accepted
Server must close connection after responding to Connection: close
Response with content should include Content-Type header
Date header should use IMF-fixdate format
Origin server must include Date header in responses
Duplicate Content-Type headers with different values
Duplicate Host headers with identical values must be rejected
Unknown Expect value should be rejected with 417
GET with Content-Length and body — semantically unusual
HEAD response must not contain a message body
Empty Host header value must be rejected
Host header with path component must be rejected
Host header with userinfo (user@host) must be rejected
HTTP/1.0 without keep-alive — server should close connection after response
HTTP/1.0 without Host header — valid per HTTP/1.0
HTTP/1.2 — higher minor version should be accepted as HTTP/1.x compatible
Leading CRLF before request-line — server may ignore per RFC
Server should accept request-lines of at least 8000 octets
Lowercase method 'get' — methods are case-sensitive per RFC
CONNECT to an origin server must be rejected
TRACE request — should be disabled in production
Server must not send 1xx responses to an HTTP/1.0 client
Server must not send Content-Length in a 204 response
OPTIONS response should include Allow header listing supported methods
OPTIONS * is the only valid asterisk-form request
POST with Content-Length and matching body must be accepted
POST with Content-Length: 10 but only 5 bytes sent — incomplete body
POST with Content-Length: 0 and no body must be accepted
POST with neither Content-Length nor Transfer-Encoding — implicit zero-length body
POST with unrecognized Content-Type — server may return 415
Invalid Range header syntax should be ignored
Range header on POST must be ignored — Range only applies to GET
Tab as request-line delimiter — SHOULD reject but MAY parse on whitespace
Whitespace inside request-target is invalid
TRACE should exclude sensitive headers from echoed response
TRACE with Content-Length body should be rejected
Unrecognized method should be rejected with 501 or 405
Unknown Transfer-Encoding without CL should be rejected with 501
HTTP version is case-sensitive — lowercase 'http' must be rejected
HTTP/01.01 — leading zeros in version digits are invalid
HTTP/1 with no minor version digit is invalid
HTTP/ 1.1 — whitespace inside version token is invalid
Whitespace before first header line must be rejected
Control characters (0x01-0x03) in cookie value — dangerous if preserved
Basic Cookie header echoed back by /echo endpoint
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 40
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie: foo=bar

description: Empty Cookie header value — tests parser resilience
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 33
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie:

description: Completely malformed cookie value (===;;;) — tests parser crash resilience
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 39
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie: ===;;;

description: 1000 cookie key=value pairs — tests parser performance limits
Two separate Cookie headers — should be folded per RFC 6265 §5.4
rawRequest:
GET /echo HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie: a=1
Cookie: b=2
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 40
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie: a=1,b=2

description: NUL byte in cookie value — dangerous if preserved by parser
64KB Cookie header — tests header size limits on cookie data
rawRequest:
GET /echo HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie: 

[Truncated — showing 8,192 of 65,595 bytes]
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 431 Request Header Fields Too Large
Connection: close

description: Basic cookie parsed correctly by framework
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 9
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

foo=bar

description: Cookie with empty value parsed without crash
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 6
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

foo=

description: Multiple cookies parsed correctly by framework
expected: 2xx with a=1, b=2, c=3 in body
rawRequest:
GET /cookie HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie: a=1; b=2; c=3
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 15
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

a=1
b=2
c=3

description: Cookies with spaces and = in values — tests framework parser edge cases
rawRequest:
GET /cookie HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1:<PORT>
Cookie: a=hello world; b=x=y
rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-length: 22
connection: keep-alive
date: <DATE>

a=hello world
b=x=y

description: Random binary garbage should be rejected or connection closed
64KB chunk extension — tests extension length limits (CVE-2023-39326 class)
Chunk size with integer overflow must be rejected
Empty Content-Length value must be rejected
Content-Length with integer overflow value must be rejected
Content-Length with tab as OWS — valid per RFC but unusual
Control characters in header value should be rejected
Zero bytes — TCP connection established without sending any data
HTTP/2 connection preface sent to HTTP/1.1 server must be rejected
Partial HTTP request — request-line and headers but no final CRLF
100KB header name should be rejected with 400/431
100KB header value should be rejected with 431
100KB method name should be rejected
100KB URL should be rejected with 414 URI Too Long
10,000 headers should be rejected with 431
Non-ASCII bytes (UTF-8 ë) in header name must be rejected
Non-ASCII bytes (UTF-8 é) in URL must be rejected
NUL byte in header value should be rejected
NUL byte in URL should be rejected
POST with Content-Length: 999999999 but no body — tests timeout vs memory allocation
1000 overlapping Range values — resource exhaustion vector (CVE-2011-3192 class)
Backslash in URL path — not valid URI character, some servers normalize to /
Overlong UTF-8 encoding of / (0xC0 0xAF) in URL must be rejected
Percent-encoded CRLF (%0d%0a) in URL — header injection if server decodes during parsing
Percent-encoded NUL byte (%00) in URL — security risk from null byte injection
Whitespace-only request line should be rejected or timeout
All-uppercase TRANSFER-ENCODING — checks if server normalizes header name casing
Space before colon in Content-Length — checks if server strips whitespace before colon
Tab character in header name — checks if server normalizes Content\tLength to Content-Length
Underscore in Content-Length name — checks if server normalizes Content_Length to Content-Length
Underscore in Transfer-Encoding name — checks if server normalizes Transfer_Encoding to Transfer-Encoding
Duplicate Host headers with different values must be rejected
Whitespace between header name and colon must be rejected
Bare LF in header should be rejected, but MAY be accepted
Bare LF in request line should be rejected, but MAY be accepted
HTTP/0.9 request (no version) must be rejected
Invalid HTTP version must be rejected
CR without LF as line ending must be rejected
Request line with no target (space but no path) must be rejected
Multiple spaces between request-line components — SHOULD reject but MAY parse leniently
Fragment (#) in request-target — not part of origin-form grammar
Empty header name (leading colon) must be rejected
Header line without colon must be rejected
Header name with invalid characters (brackets) must be rejected
Obs-fold (line folding) in headers should be rejected
Non-numeric Content-Length must be rejected
Content-Length with plus sign must be rejected
Request without Host header must be rejected with 400
Absolute-form URI with different Host header — routing confusion vector
Bare CR in header value must be rejected or replaced with SP
Chunk size line terminated by bare CR — not a valid line terminator
Chunk size with bare semicolon and no extension name must be rejected
Bare CR (not CRLF) in chunk extension — some parsers treat CR alone as line ending
NUL byte in chunk extension must be rejected
Chunk extension with invalid token character must be rejected
Bare LF in chunk extension — server MAY accept bare LF per RFC 9112 §2.2
Chunk size with 0x prefix must be rejected
Invalid chunk size (+0) with poison byte — detects chunk-size parser desync
Chunk size with leading space must be rejected
Bare LF as chunk data terminator — server MAY accept bare LF per RFC 9112 §2.2
Bare LF in chunked trailer termination — server MAY accept bare LF per RFC 9112 §2.2
Chunk data without trailing CRLF must be rejected
Negative chunk size must be rejected
Chunk size with leading plus sign must be rejected
Chunk size with trailing whitespace before CRLF must be rejected
Chunk declares size 5 but sends 7 bytes — oversized chunk data must be rejected
Chunk size with underscores (1_0) must be rejected
Transfer-Encoding: chunked;ext=val — parameters on chunked encoding
Content-Length with comma-separated different values must be rejected
Content-Length with comma-separated identical values — some servers merge
Content-Length with three comma-separated identical values — extended merge test
Content-Length: 00 — matches 1*DIGIT but leading zero ambiguity
Content-Length with extra leading whitespace (double space OWS)
Content-Length with hex prefix (0x5) must be rejected
Content-Length with internal space (1 0) must be rejected
Content-Length with leading zeros — valid per 1*DIGIT grammar but may cause parser disagreement
Content-Length: 0200 — octal 128 vs decimal 200, parser disagreement vector
Negative Content-Length must be rejected
Content-Length: -0 must be rejected — not valid 1*DIGIT
Content-Length with octal prefix (0o5) must be rejected
Both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding present — server MAY reject or process with TE alone
Content-Length with trailing space — OWS trimming is valid per RFC 9110 §5.5
Content-Length with underscore digit separator (1_0) must be rejected
Content-Length: 0 with trailing bytes — checks if leftover bytes poison the next request
CL+TE conflict — server MUST close connection after responding
CL.TE desync — leftover bytes after the body boundary may be interpreted as the next request
CL.TE conflict — both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding: chunked present
CL.TE desync — embedded GET in body; multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
CL.TE desync with non-numeric Content-Length (N<alpha>) — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
CL.TE desync with malformed Content-Length (+N) — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
CL.TE desync with TE case mismatch — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
CL.TE desync with TE leading comma — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
CL.TE desync with obs-folded Transfer-Encoding — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
CL.TE desync with TE trailing space — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
CL.TE desync — embedded HEAD in body; multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
Duplicate Content-Length with different values must be rejected
CL.CL ambiguity with embedded GET — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
Expect: 100-continue with Content-Length — server should send 100 then read body
Expect: 100-continue with immediate body followed by a second request — detects unread-body desync
GET with Content-Length body followed by a second request — detects unread-body desync
GET with Content-Length body containing an incomplete request prefix — follow-up completes it if body was left unread
expected: 400/close preferred; extra response on step 2 = warn

description: HEAD request with Content-Length and body — server must not leave body on connection
Host header with comma-separated values must be rejected
OPTIONS with Content-Length and body — server should consume or reject body
OPTIONS with Content-Length body followed by a second request — detects unread-body desync
OPTIONS with TE obs-fold and CL present — server must reject or close after response
Baseline — two clean GET requests on one keep-alive connection
Transfer-Encoding: Chunked (capital C) with CL — case-insensitive is valid
Transfer-Encoding: chunked, chunked with CL is ambiguous
Two Transfer-Encoding headers with CL present — ambiguous framing
TE.TE + CL ambiguity with embedded GET — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
Transfer-Encoding with empty value must be rejected
Form feed before 'chunked' in TE value — control char obfuscation vector
Transfer-Encoding in HTTP/1.0 request must be rejected
Transfer-Encoding: identity (deprecated) with CL must be rejected
Transfer-Encoding with leading comma (, chunked) — RFC says empty list elements MUST be ignored
Transfer-Encoding where chunked is not final — server MUST respond with 400 (RFC 9112 §6.3)
NUL byte appended to 'chunked' in TE value — C-string truncation attack
Transfer-Encoding with obs-fold line wrapping must be rejected
Transfer-Encoding with space before colon must be rejected
Transfer-Encoding with tab as OWS before value
Transfer-Encoding: chunked, — trailing comma produces empty list element
Transfer-Encoding: 'chunked ' (trailing space) must not be treated as chunked
Vertical tab before 'chunked' in TE value — control char obfuscation vector
Transfer-Encoding: xchunked must not be treated as chunked
TE+CL conflict (reversed order) — server MUST close connection after responding
TE.CL desync — chunked terminator before CL boundary, leftover bytes smuggled
TE.CL conflict — Transfer-Encoding: chunked + conflicting Content-Length
TE.CL desync via chunk-size prefix trick — multiple responses indicate request boundary confusion
Authorization header in chunked trailers — prohibited per RFC 9110 §6.5.1
Content-Length in chunked trailers must be ignored — prohibited trailer field
Content-Type in chunked trailer — prohibited per RFC 9110 §6.5.1
Host header in chunked trailers must not be used for routing
Transfer-Encoding in chunked trailers must be ignored — prohibited trailer field
Transfer_Encoding (underscore) header with CL — not a valid header but some parsers accept
Upgrade header in HTTP/1.0 request must be ignored
WebSocket upgrade with unsupported version — should return 426
Upgrade header without Connection: Upgrade must not trigger protocol switch
WebSocket upgrade via POST must not be accepted
Upgrade to unknown protocol must not return 101
